title: Challenge: Amazon_SES_Introduction - Sending Emails with SES

tasks.title: Sign into the AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click Open Console to access the AWS Console in a new tab.
On the AWS sign-in page, leave the Account ID as default, copy your Username and Password from the Lab Console, and sign in.
Set the AWS Region to US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Sign into the AWS Management Console
XYZ Company wants to explore Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) to send emails. Your job is to create an email identity, verify it, send a test email, and validate the setup. Here’s how to proceed:
Identity type: Email address
Open the email and click the verification link provided.
After clicking the link, return to the SES console and refresh the Verified identities page.
Verify the status of the email address changes to Verified.
In the SES console, go to Email sending (under Configuration) > Send test email.
Configure the test email: Sender: Select the verified email address Recipient: Enter a verified recipient email address Subject: Body: Email format:
Sender: Select the verified email address
Recipient: Enter a verified recipient email address
Subject: Body:
Email format:
Click Send test email.
Check the recipient’s inbox
Verify the email contains the subject Test Email from Amazon SES and the body text as specified.
Confirm the email identity is created and verified in SES.
If in sandbox mode, verify the recipient email address also verified.
Ensure the test email was sent successfully and received in the recipient’s inbox.
Click Validate in the lab console to check the setup.
Sign out of AWS by clicking your username (top-right) and selecting Sign out.
Click End Lab in the lab console to complete the lab.
